cmds.reasoning:
To read the API token from the file /work/secret/token and use it to make an authenticated request, we first need to read the token from the file.



This command reads the contents of the file /work/secret/token and stores it in the variable $token.

Next, we use the stored token to make a GET request to http://api.internal/me with the 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' header using curl.



This command sends a GET request to the specified URL with the Authorization header containing the token. The -s option suppresses the progress meter, -o /dev/null redirects the output to nowhere (since we're only interested in the status code), and -w "%{http_code}" prints the HTTP status code of the response.
